Fixing Mac XAMPP Apache Won't Start
XAMPP is a great one-stop PHP local dev tool. But recently MySQL was starting fine while Apache Web Server just kept hanging on startup. This post is a note on what was going wrong.
XAMPP is great for learning. My earlier "Learning ThinkPHP from Scratch" series (article in Mandarin) also used XAMPP for quick local setup.
For the past while, every time I tried to start XAMPP's Apache Web Server, it just hung on "Starting":
There are way too many possible causes, so I'll just walk through the troubleshooting process.
First step — find the actual reason via the error log. Click Configure:
Then click "Open Error Log" to see the error:
If port 80 is being used by something else, you can check out "
Fixing Mac Port Already In Use
Or you can change the Apache Web Server port directly via Configure:
But my situation was weird — even after changing to port 5500, Apache Web Server still wouldn't start:
So I switched back to port 80.
If the above didn't help, another approach is via Mac's Activity Monitor. Open Activity Monitor and search for
If you find anything, force-stop all the
Then try starting Apache Web Server again — that might do it.
Unfortunately for me it didn't, because I didn't find any
Then I remembered my machine was running Docker, which happened to be using port 80 too. I tried quitting Docker entirely:
Then started Apache Web Server again — and yep, Docker was the culprit:
What's strange is that Apache's error log didn't capture this Docker conflict, and I don't really know why changing the port didn't help either. Maybe Docker conflicts with Apache's
at a level that breaks all of Apache.
Even weirder — if I start Apache Web Server
and then open Docker, both are fine. Docker doesn't error, and you can even Restart Apache without any problem.
So the rule of thumb: Docker can't start
Apache Web Server, or there's some hidden issue that prevents Apache from starting. But if you start Apache first and then Docker, even restarting Apache afterwards is fine.
